meta.title: Steps to Calories Calculator — 10000 Steps in Calories & km
meta.description: How many calories does 10000 steps burn? How far is 10000 steps in km? Calculate calories burned, distance walked, and your activity level — free, no sign-up.

schritteKalorienRechner.title: Steps to Calories Calculator
schritteKalorienRechner.description: Convert daily step count into calories burned, distance covered, and activity level.
schritteKalorienRechner.strideHint: Optional. Estimated from height, or 75 cm by default.

activityHints.sedentary: < 5000 steps/day — predominantly sitting lifestyle. Goal: gradually push toward 7500+.
activityHints.low: 5000–7499 steps/day — low active. Short walks bump this up easily.
activityHints.moderate: 7500–9999 steps/day — somewhat active. You're on track toward 10000.
activityHints.active: 10000–12499 steps/day — active. Meets WHO physical activity recommendation.
activityHints.veryActive: ≥ 12500 steps/day — highly active. Comparable to physically demanding jobs or sport.

schritteKalorienRechner.incompleteHint: Enter steps and weight to calculate calories and distance.
schritteKalorienRechner.exampleText: 10000 × 0.04 × (80 / 70) ≈ 457 kcal. With a 75 cm stride that's 7.5 km. Activity level: active.
schritteKalorienRechner.howItWorksText: The rule of thumb kcal ≈ steps × 0.04 × (weight / 70) is based on the Compendium of Physical Activities and Tudor-Locke et al. (2008). 0.04 kcal/step is the mid-range energy cost for moderate walking (~5 km/h) at 70 kg. The value scales linearly with weight — heavier walkers burn more per step. Stride length is roughly 41 % of body height; 75 cm is a solid default for adults.
schritteKalorienRechner.disclaimer: Estimates. Actual burn depends on walking speed, incline, surface and individual efficiency. ±15 % deviation is normal.

faq.q: How many calories does 10000 steps burn?
faq.a: About 400 kcal at 70 kg, 457 kcal at 80 kg, and 514 kcal at 90 kg. The rule of thumb kcal ≈ steps × 0.04 × (weight / 70) holds for moderate walking at ~5 km/h.

faq.q: How far is 10000 steps in km?
faq.a: At an average stride of 75 cm, 10000 steps is 7.5 km. With a 70 cm stride: 7.0 km. With an 80 cm stride: 8.0 km. Stride length is roughly 41 % of body height.

faq.q: Are 10000 steps a day really necessary?
faq.a: The 10000 figure traces back to a 1965 Japanese marketing campaign — research finds health benefits already at 7000–8000 daily steps. More is better; 10000 is a goal, not a hard threshold.
The rule of thumb has a typical ±15 % deviation. Higher precision requires individual measurement (e.g. indirect calorimetry) — for everyday tracking the estimate is more than enough.

faq.q: Does walking uphill change the result?
faq.a: Substantially. Walking uphill can raise calorie burn by 50–100 %. The rule of thumb assumes flat terrain — adjust upward on inclines.

faq.q: What about slow or fast walking?
faq.a: Strolling (< 3 km/h) burns roughly 20 % less; brisk walking or fitness walking (> 6 km/h) up to 30 % more than the rule of thumb.
Adults walk with a 65–85 cm stride. Women: ~67 cm, men: ~75 cm. Quick estimate: height × 0.41.
